title: Podman for Windows: machine init fails
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

The machine Init fails:

```powershell
podman machine init --image-path C:\Users\user\Downloads\podman-rootfs.tar.xz
Error: open C:\Users\user\.local\share\containers\podman\machine\wsl\cache: is a directory
```

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install Podman using the msi noted below.

2. Start a PowerShell session as non Administrative User

3. Issuing the follwoing podam command:
 
```powershell
podman machine init --image-path C:\Users\user\Downloads\podman-rootfs.tar.xz
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I have also tried to remove the folder structure under `C:\Users\user\.local\share\containers\podman\*`, but without success. :(

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman.exe version 4.2.0
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
is not useful because machine init fails
```

**Used Package Version and Machine Image:**

```
podman-v4.2.0.msi (sha256hash: 40d506d04b32410da3ac0d570c50fb84b3a6e5157fa131bc8cb66c410cb463e2)
podman-rootfs.tar.xz (sha256hash: 1f45b65128df25b3ec54941e77c353eebc398a64e96b3c63871d6e74349265d1)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
WindowsCurrentVersion                                   : 6.3
WindowsEditionId                                        : Enterprise
WindowsInstallationType                                 : Client
WindowsProductName                                      : Windows 10 Enterprise
WindowsSystemRoot                                       : C:\WINDOWS
WindowsVersion                                          : 2009
CsSystemType                                            : x64-based PC
```

Thanks for your great work!
bittacker

comments.body: @n1hility Thanks for taking on this issue. Please let me know if you need any further information.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm seeing the exact same issue.  Any updates / hints / workarounds?

And some more context...

I see the same issue on Podman 4.2.0 and 4.3.0.  Executing `podman machine init` without the image-path flag works fine.

This may not be the case for the original post, but for me this only happens when my Windows image path has a space in it.

`podman machine init --image-path "C:\Path with Spaces\rootfs.tar.xz"`

And yes I'll stop posting updates now...  😄
@w60001 i believe this is a bug with fully qualified path handling, you should be able to workaround with a relative path ´—image-path .\rootfs.tar.gs´

I have just tried `podman.exe version 4.3.1` and everything works as expected.

Thanks fixing this :)

> @w60001 i believe this is a bug with fully qualified path handling, you should be able to workaround with a relative path ´—image-path .\rootfs.tar.gs´

I tried with podman 4.3.1, it didn't work with the fully qualified path. But it worked with relative path. Thank you a lot
